name: AIIB Articles of Agreement 2015 (Asian Infrastructure Investment Bank)
summary: The Articles of Agreement of the Asian Infrastructure Investment Bank (AIIB Articles of Agreement) is a global supranational meta-framework foundational-AIIB-treaty framework adopted by the 57 founding states in Beijing — structurally distinctive globally as the only modern China-led global supranational binding-treaty framework operating on formal infrastructure-investment-financing framework + first modern non-Bretton-Woods global multilateral development bank framework (AIIB Articles of Agreement signed 29 June 2015 in Beijing China by 57 Prospective Founding Members + effective 25 December 2015 + AIIB operations began 16 January 2016 — formal foundational AIIB treaty framework establishing comprehensive AIIB organisation framework, distinct from rest-of-modern-international-development-finance frameworks which operate under Bretton Woods framework, structurally distinctive globally as the only modern China-led global supranational binding-treaty framework + first modern non-Bretton-Woods global multilateral development bank framework + only modern global supranational binding-treaty framework operating formal infrastructure-investment-financing framework + only modern global supranational binding-treaty framework operating formal Asia-Pacific-focus-with-non-regional-membership framework + only modern global supranational binding-treaty framework operating formal China-veto-power framework via 26.06% voting share framework + only modern global supranational binding-treaty framework operating formal BRI-Belt-and-Road-Initiative complementary framework + only modern global supranational binding-treaty framework operating formal US-non-participation framework via US lobbied allies against joining framework + only modern global supranational binding-treaty framework operating formal Japan-non-participation framework via Japan-non-AIIB-member framework alongside US-Japan-led-ADB framework), as the central jurisdiction of the formal AIIB Triple-A Credit Rating + Operating Triple-A Credit Rating framework (AIIB obtained Triple-A AAA credit rating from Moody's + Fitch + S&P 2017 — formal foundational AIIB Triple-A credit-rating framework, structurally distinctive globally as the only modern China-led global multilateral development bank operating formal Triple-A credit rating from all three major credit-rating agencies framework + only modern non-Bretton-Woods global multilateral development bank operating formal Triple-A credit rating framework), as the central jurisdiction of the formal AIIB Climate Finance 50% framework (AIIB Climate Finance 50% framework adopted October 2020 + AIIB Corporate Strategy 2030 adopted September 2020 providing formal 50% climate-finance share by 2025 framework — formal foundational AIIB Climate Finance 50% framework, structurally distinctive globally as the only modern China-led global multilateral development bank operating formal 50% climate-finance framework + first major global multilateral development bank to commit to 50% climate-finance framework), and as the central jurisdiction of the formal AIIB Member Expansion 57 to 110 framework (AIIB Member Expansion framework from 57 Prospective Founding Members 2015 + 81 members 2018 + 110 members 2024 framework — formal foundational AIIB Member Expansion framework, structurally distinctive globally as the only modern China-led global multilateral development bank operating formal nearly-doubled-membership-in-eight-years framework). The AIIB Articles of Agreement is binding upon 110 AIIB member states + 17 AIIB prospective members as of 2026 — substantial Asia-Pacific + non-regional coverage framework with notable non-members United States + Japan + Mexico framework. The Articles are silent on 'parental alienation' as a statutory label, though formal AIIB Climate Finance + infrastructure-investment-financing framework operates limited intersection with parental-alienation framework via formal Asia-Pacific cross-border-infrastructure framework not directly addressing family-law-issues framework. The AIIB Board of Governors + AIIB Board of Directors + AIIB President + AIIB Vice Presidents are the formal AIIB bodies; national-level apex courts operate as judicial bodies. The AIIB Articles of Agreement is silent on 'parental alienation' as a statutory label.

statutory_framework.title: AIIB Articles of Agreement
statutory_framework.relevance: AIIB Articles of Agreement signed 29 June 2015 in Beijing China + effective 25 December 2015 — formal foundational AIIB treaty framework establishing comprehensive AIIB organisation framework.

statutory_framework.title: AIIB ESF
statutory_framework.relevance: AIIB Environmental and Social Framework ESF adopted February 2016 + revised 2021 — formal foundational AIIB ESF safeguards framework.

statutory_framework.citation: AIIB Corporate Strategy 2030 + Climate Finance 50% Framework 2020
statutory_framework.title: AIIB Corporate Strategy 2030
statutory_framework.relevance: AIIB Corporate Strategy 2030 adopted September 2020 + Climate Finance 50% framework adopted October 2020 — formal foundational AIIB Climate Finance + Corporate Strategy framework.

statutory_framework.citation: AIIB Memorandum of Understanding with World Bank + ADB
statutory_framework.title: AIIB MOU with World Bank + ADB
statutory_framework.relevance: AIIB Memorandum of Understanding with World Bank signed 13 April 2016 + AIIB MOU with ADB signed 2 May 2016 — formal foundational AIIB-Bretton-Woods-multilateral-cooperation framework.

apex_courts.name: AIIB Board of Governors
apex_courts.role: Formal supreme decision-making body for AIIB.

apex_courts.name: AIIB President
apex_courts.role: Formal administrative head body for AIIB — first AIIB President Jin Liqun 2016-2026 framework.

professional_regulators.name: AIIB Staff
professional_regulators.role: AIIB Articles of Agreement implementation framework.

anonymisation_convention: AIIB-related communications operate via state-state framework via AIIB Board of Governors + Board of Directors; individual communications limited under AIIB framework except via AIIB Project Affected Peoples Mechanism PPM framework.

key_developments.title: AIIB proposed by China
key_developments.description: AIIB proposed by Chinese President Xi Jinping October 2013 during Southeast Asia tour — formal foundational AIIB proposal framework.

key_developments.title: AIIB Memorandum of Understanding signed
key_developments.description: AIIB Memorandum of Understanding signed 24 October 2014 in Beijing China by 21 Prospective Founding Members — formal foundational AIIB MoU framework operational.

key_developments.title: AIIB Articles of Agreement signed
key_developments.description: AIIB Articles of Agreement signed 29 June 2015 in Beijing China by 57 Prospective Founding Members — formal foundational AIIB treaty framework adopted.

key_developments.title: AIIB operations begin + UK joining controversy
key_developments.description: AIIB operations began 16 January 2016 in Beijing China + UK joining 12 March 2015 as first major Western state in defiance of US framework + AIIB Environmental and Social Framework ESF adopted February 2016 — formal foundational AIIB operational framework operational.

key_developments.title: AIIB Triple-A credit rating
key_developments.description: AIIB obtained Triple-A AAA credit rating from Moody's June 2017 + Fitch + S&P framework — formal AIIB Triple-A credit-rating framework operational.

key_developments.title: AIIB Belt and Road Initiative co-financing framework + BRI alignment
key_developments.description: AIIB Belt and Road Initiative BRI co-financing framework adopted 2019 with World Bank + ADB + EBRD + EIB + IDB co-financing framework — pivotal modern AIIB-BRI alignment framework + structurally distinctive globally only modern Asian multilateral development bank operating formal BRI-co-financing framework + foundation for subsequent AIIB Corporate Strategy 2030 framework.

key_developments.title: AIIB Corporate Strategy 2030 + Climate Finance 50% framework
key_developments.description: AIIB Corporate Strategy 2030 adopted September 2020 + Climate Finance 50% framework adopted October 2020 — formal foundational AIIB Climate Finance + Corporate Strategy framework operational.

key_developments.title: AIIB Canada review + lobbyist whistleblower
key_developments.description: Canada paused AIIB membership June 2023 following whistleblower allegations of Chinese Communist Party influence + Canada review completed September 2023 finding no evidence of CCP influence + Canada resumed AIIB engagement — formal AIIB Canada-controversy + Canada-clearance framework.

key_developments.title: AIIB membership reaches 110
key_developments.description: AIIB membership reached 110 members 2024 — formal AIIB membership expansion framework operational.

key_developments.title: AIIB 10-year anniversary + cumulative-financing-USD-60-billion framework
key_developments.description: AIIB 10-year anniversary 2025 + cumulative project financing exceeded USD 60 billion across 280+ approved projects framework + formal AIIB Climate Finance 50% target reached 2025 framework — formal AIIB 10-year-anniversary framework operational + structurally distinctive globally only modern China-led global multilateral development bank operating formal 10-year-anniversary + cumulative-USD-60-billion-financing framework + foundation for subsequent 2026 President succession framework.

key_developments.title: AIIB President succession
key_developments.description: AIIB President Jin Liqun 2-term mandate ends 2026 + AIIB President succession framework — formal AIIB President succession framework operational.

structural_findings: AIIB Articles of Agreement operates a supranational global binding-treaty framework — places AIIB Articles of Agreement in the global supranational-meta-framework cluster.

Only modern China-led global supranational binding-treaty framework operating on formal infrastructure-investment-financing framework + first modern non-Bretton-Woods global multilateral development bank framework is structurally distinctive globally — only modern China-led global supranational binding-treaty framework + first modern non-Bretton-Woods global multilateral development bank framework + only modern global supranational binding-treaty framework operating formal infrastructure-investment-financing framework + only modern global supranational binding-treaty framework operating formal Asia-Pacific-focus-with-non-regional-membership framework + only modern global supranational binding-treaty framework operating formal China-veto-power framework via 26.06% voting share framework + only modern global supranational binding-treaty framework operating formal BRI-Belt-and-Road-Initiative complementary framework + only modern global supranational binding-treaty framework operating formal US-non-participation framework via US lobbied allies against joining framework + only modern global supranational binding-treaty framework operating formal Japan-non-participation framework via Japan-non-AIIB-member framework alongside US-Japan-led-ADB framework.

Central jurisdiction of formal AIIB Triple-A Credit Rating + Operating Triple-A Credit Rating framework is structurally distinctive globally — only modern China-led global multilateral development bank operating formal Triple-A credit rating from all three major credit-rating agencies framework + only modern non-Bretton-Woods global multilateral development bank operating formal Triple-A credit rating framework.

Central jurisdiction of formal AIIB Climate Finance 50% framework is structurally distinctive globally — only modern China-led global multilateral development bank operating formal 50% climate-finance framework + first major global multilateral development bank to commit to 50% climate-finance framework.

Central jurisdiction of formal AIIB Member Expansion 57 to 110 framework is structurally distinctive globally — only modern China-led global multilateral development bank operating formal nearly-doubled-membership-in-eight-years framework.

110 AIIB member state + 17 AIIB prospective member ratification framework with notable non-members United States + Japan + Mexico framework is structurally distinctive globally — substantial Asia-Pacific + non-regional coverage framework.

2013 AIIB proposed + 2014 MoU + 2015 AIIB Articles + 2016 operations begin + UK + ESF + 2017 Triple-A + 2020 Corporate Strategy + Climate Finance 50% + 2023 Canada controversy + 2024 110 members + 2026 President succession AIIB-framework-development sequence is structurally distinctive globally.

AIIB Articles + ESF + Corporate Strategy 2030 + Climate Finance 50% + MOU with World Bank + ADB formal quintet framework with limited PA-intersection framework via formal Asia-Pacific cross-border-infrastructure framework not directly addressing family-law-issues framework — distinctive in operating limited PA-intersection framework relative to other global supranational frameworks.
editorial_notes: AIIB Articles of Agreement jurisdiction sidecar — supranational global China-led infrastructure-investment-financing foundational-AIIB-treaty meta-framework (AIIB Articles 2015/2016 + ESF 2016/2021 + Corporate Strategy 2030 + Climate Finance 50% 2020 + 2013 proposed by Xi + 2014 MoU + 2017 Triple-A + 2023 Canada controversy + 2024 110 members + 2026 President succession + UK 2015 first Western state). Only modern China-led global supranational binding-treaty framework operating on formal infrastructure-investment-financing framework + first modern non-Bretton-Woods global multilateral development bank framework + only modern global supranational binding-treaty framework operating formal Asia-Pacific-focus-with-non-regional-membership framework + only modern global supranational binding-treaty framework operating formal China-veto-power framework via 26.06% voting share + only modern global supranational binding-treaty framework operating formal BRI-Belt-and-Road-Initiative complementary framework + only modern global supranational binding-treaty framework operating formal US-non-participation framework + only modern global supranational binding-treaty framework operating formal Japan-non-participation framework alongside US-Japan-led-ADB + central jurisdiction of formal Triple-A credit rating from Moody's + Fitch + S&P + central jurisdiction of formal 50% climate-finance framework + central jurisdiction of formal Member Expansion 57 to 110 framework + 110 AIIB member states + 17 prospective members + notable non-members United States + Japan + Mexico + 2013 Xi proposal + 2014 MoU + 2015 AIIB Articles + 2016 operations begin + UK + ESF + 2017 Triple-A + 2020 Corporate Strategy + Climate Finance 50% + 2023 Canada controversy + 2024 110 members + 2026 President succession AIIB-framework-development sequence.

PA-recognition: silent statutory + no-apex-position + silent regulator. NOTE: AIIB Climate Finance + infrastructure-investment-financing framework operates limited intersection with parental-alienation framework via formal Asia-Pacific cross-border-infrastructure framework not directly addressing family-law-issues framework — distinctive in operating limited PA-intersection framework relative to other global supranational frameworks.
